ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Thosai/Dosa. Nearest parent is a fermented grain pancake or crepe. A new FoodOn concept is recommended.
ontology.foodex2_description: Fermented grain products — fermented rice and lentil pancake, plain (closest parent)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single plain thosai weighs approximately 100g and consists of a thin, lacy, slightly crisp fermented rice and urad dal crepe with a pale golden exterior, served with coconut chutney and sambar.

nutrient_source.title: Plain Dosa (Thosai) — HPB Singapore Foodo Database, Food Code F05010009
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Exact match found in HPB Foodo under food code F05010009 — Plain Thosai (dosa), standard preparation without filling.

gi_source: University of Sydney GI Database — plain dosa (fermented rice pancake, griddled); GI higher than expected due to the gelatinised rice starch.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium approximately 185mg per thosai (100g) — about 8% of the 2300mg daily recommended limit; one of the lowest-sodium carbohydrate staples in Singapore's hawker landscape.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension may consume plain thosai freely; the coconut chutney and sambar served alongside are the main sodium contributors and can be moderated.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Plain dosa has a relatively high GI (~68) for a fermented product; however, its per-piece glycaemic load (~19) is manageable; fermentation reduces the glycaemic impact compared to non-fermented rice.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Diabetic patients can consume 1–2 plain thosai per meal, ideally with sambar and chutney which add protein and fibre; the small per-piece portion keeps the glycaemic load moderate.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Completely vegan and anti-inflammatory; fermented batter promotes gut microbiome health which supports immune regulation; urad dal provides folate and plant protein.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Plain thosai is an excellent choice for patients with rheumatoid arthritis; the fermented preparation, gluten-free composition, and vegan profile make it anti-inflammatory, and it pairs well with anti-inflammatory sambar for a complete meal.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Plain Thosai (Dosa) is the foundational South Indian fermented rice and urad dal crepe, made from a batter of soaked rice and dehusked black lentils that is fermented overnight until naturally sour and lightly effervescent, then ladled onto a hot flat griddle and spread in circular motions to create a large, thin, lacy crepe. The plain version is golden-brown, crisp at the edges, and soft in the centre, served with coconut chutney and sambar. It is gluten-free, vegan, and one of the most widely consumed South Indian breakfast items at Singapore's Indian restaurants and mamak stalls.
common_user_queries: how many calories in a plain thosai
thosai vs roti prata which is healthier

confidence_reason: Exact match found in HPB Singapore Foodo Database under food code F05010009 — Plain Thosai (dosa), standard preparation.
notes: Fully vegan. No animal products. GI is higher than expected for a fermented product because the rice starch gelatinisation during cooking largely offsets the acid-mediated GI reduction from fermentation. Cholesterol and vitamin B12 are 0.0. The coconut chutney and sambar accompaniments are not included in per_100g values.
